subject: English
chapter_name: The Case of the Fifth Word
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the mystery story involving Encyclopedia Brown
Identify the main characters and events in the story
Develop logical thinking and problem-solving skills
Understand the importance of observation and reasoning
Learn how coded messages can hide information
Improve reading comprehension and vocabulary skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Mystery Stories
topics_covered: Meaning of mystery and detective stories
Role of observation in solving mysteries
teaching_strategy: Reading the introduction of the chapter
student_activities: Share examples of mystery stories they know
Discuss qualities of a good detective

lesson_plan.title: Characters and Story Background
topics_covered: Introduction to Tim Nolan and Daniel Davenport
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the background events
student_activities: Discuss their roles in the story

lesson_plan.title: The Mystery Begins
topics_covered: The mysterious note found with the will
teaching_strategy: Teacher narration of the story events
Discussion on why the note was suspicious
student_activities: Explain why the case puzzled the police
Discuss what clues detectives should look for

lesson_plan.title: Understanding the Code
topics_covered: The coded words 'Nom Utes Sweden Hurts'
Connection between the words and days of the week
Role of the missing fifth word
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the coded message
Demonstration using days of the week

lesson_plan.title: Solving the Mystery
topics_covered: The clue about the fir tree
student_activities: Explain how the code helped solve the mystery
Discuss why logical thinking is important

lesson_plan.title: Language and Revision Activities
student_activities: Convert direct speech to indirect speech

teacher_tip: Encourage students to think like detectives by analysing clues and patterns in the coded message. Let them attempt to decode the message before revealing the answer.
